events.title: The DuPage County Fair
events.description: The DuPage County Fair July 23-26, 2026 The DuPage County Fair is one of Wheaton’s highlighted community events. We love seeing all community members come together to enjoy quality time with their neighbors, friends, and family. events.title: Bee Curious: Up Close & Un-bee-lievable
events.description: Join us in celebration of World Bee Day with scientists from the Negaunee Integrative Research and Keller Science Action Centers! This engaging walk-in session dives into the fascinating world of global and local pollinators, focusing on the un-bee-lievable ones and the role they play in keeping our ecosystems blooming. events.title: Armour Seminar: Scott Weidensaul
events.description: At this week's talk, Ornithologist and author Scott Weidensaul discusses his book The Return of the Oystercatcher: Saving Birds to Save the Planet.

events.title: Poetry Pop-up: Animal Theater
events.description: Imagine what it would feel like to stand next to a T. rex. Ponder what flowers smelled like 30 million years ago. See what inspiration you'll find and start turning your ideas into poetry with Eric Elshtain, the Field Museum's first poet in residence. Join us for a series of weekly events that change how you see the museum—whether you’ve visited once or doze

events.title: Meet a Scientist: Our Cosmic Origins
events.description: View extraterrestrial materials from the Field Museum's collection and explore how scientists use them to learn more about our cosmic origin story. Meet Philipp R. events.title: Jewish Botany: Plant Medicine and Magic in the Jewish Diaspora
events.description: Celebrate Jewish American Heritage Month! Come view botanical specimens related to the Jewish diaspora and learn about how healers utilized common household plants throughout Eastern Europe to stave off everyday ailments, as well as protect themselves from curses. events.title: PlayLab PlayDate: Fossil Fun Fest

events.title: James Hutton, the father of Modern Geology, turns 300 years old
events.description: Learn about James Hutton and how angular unconformities teach us about deep time, and how modern processes going on now teach us about the rock cycle. Presenters include Paul Mayer, Fossil Invertebrate Collections Manager, and James Holstein, Geology Collections Manager

events.title: ¡Tocamos! Oaxacan Folk Art by Avelino Pérez
events.description: The Field Museum is proud to showcase new sculptures from the collection by Oaxacan artist Avelino Pérez. These beautifully carved and painted figures were created in collaboration with the bilingual children's book author, Cynthia Weill, for her latest publication ¡Tocamos! View our newest acquisitions from Oaxaca and learn about the Field Muse

events.title: PlayLab PlayDate: Juneteenth Celebration
events.description: Families with children ages 2–6 are invited to a free, drop-in PlayDate to celebrate Juneteenth. This PlayDate will feature stories and a craft celebrating the liberation of enslaved Black Americans. Join us for a fun-filled PlayDate with stories and more!

events.title: Drawing Inspiration: Art in the Gardens
events.description: Everyone is invited to come observe and participate in Art in the Gardens! Field Museum staff and volunteers who dabble in the arts professionally or for fun will be scattered throughout Rice Native Gardens – the Museum’s largest and free outdoor exhibit, creating art inspired by the gardens in a variety of mediums. events.title: Nectar Feeders vs. Insect Hunters: A Closer Look at Bats
events.description: Celebrate Pollinator Week with scientists from the Gantz Family Collections Center! Bats are often celebrated as important nighttime pollinators, but not all bats play that role. events.title: What Insects Call Our Garden Home (and Maybe Yours Too!)
events.description: Feel empowered to be a scientist! Check out equipment scientists use in the field to observe and document insects and see photos and specimens from the insect collections.
